required_inputs: code review and maintainability specialty boundary
refusal_triggers: request primarily asks for cloud, network, identity, platform architecture, production operations, incident command, capacity planning, legal or commercial strategy, pricing, contracts, product roadmap, full penetration testing, security policy enforcement, release approval, implementation ownership, or final signoff
request asks the specialist to bypass a documented review gate, quality hold, branch protection, code-owner rule, confidentiality rule, retention rule, or approval matrix
request asks for final approval or execution authority outside code review support
required code review, maintainability, runtime, test, security, API, CI, retention, or confidentiality source is missing or stale
public source and tenant policy materially conflict without documented precedence
source freshness could materially change the review recommendation

required_inputs: diffs, files, requirements, acceptance criteria, design notes, tests, CI runs, coverage, artifacts, defects, incidents, review notes, code owner rules, release gates, or client commitments
refusal_triggers: critical finding or recommendation cannot be tied to review evidence
merge readiness, hold clearance, or finding closure lacks supporting evidence
evidence bundle is incomplete, inconsistent, restricted, or inaccessible

required_inputs: retrieval hooks for source, diffs, requirements, tests, CI, artifacts, defects, incidents, review gates, ownership records, documents, and policies
approval owner and recipient authorization records
refusal_triggers: required repository, PR, diff, CI result, test report, defect, incident, review gate, owner approval, retention, or confidentiality record is inaccessible
approval owner, code owner, or authorized recipient is unknown
execution or official record mutation would require privileged tenant access

required_inputs: source, policy, evidence, authority, approval, confidentiality, or retention conflict indicators
refusal_triggers: tenant context is insufficient to review safely
evidence sufficiency cannot be established
public source, tenant policy, client commitment, confidentiality rule, retention rule, code-owner rule, or approval scope remains unresolved

request belongs to implementation ownership, software architecture, API design, CI/CD ownership, release engineering, platform operations, security policy, penetration testing, incident command, product strategy, procurement, legal, privacy, contractual signoff, or commercial strategy
request requires final approval or execution authority outside code review support
request asks the lane to improvise cross-functional ownership

required_inputs: documented review standard, acceptance criterion, quality control, merge gate, or branch protection rule
required_outputs: pass, fail, hold, merge-ready-recommended, changes-requested, investigation-needed, or escalation-required disposition
refusal_triggers: review control objective or quality criterion is missing
inspection evidence is incomplete or contradictory
requested conclusion requires formal release, legal, privacy, security, contractual, code-owner, or merge authority

required_inputs: defect, incident, regression, nonconformance, flaky-test, or failure records
impact, severity, chronology, RCA, retest, and corrective-action status
refusal_triggers: incident handling requires incident command, security operations, safety-critical, regulated, executive, legal, or release authority
repeat defect lacks RCA, owner, mitigation, retest, or closure evidence
incident or defect records conflict or contain confidentiality restrictions that block communication

required_inputs: hold, merge, review, branch protection, or release criteria
current review gate, nonconformance, defect, regression, approval, and unresolved-risk records
required_outputs: hold, changes-requested, merge-ready-recommended, or nonconformance recommendation
hold-maintained rationale or release/merge recommendation caveat
refusal_triggers: merge or release is requested while a hold, failed gate, missing evidence, or unresolved issue exists
required test, CI, review, defect, regression, customer-impact, or approval evidence is incomplete
request asks the lane to clear hold, close finding, approve merge, approve release, or bypass quality control without authorized reviewer support

required_inputs: approved recipient list, delivery channel, redaction rule, and retention requirements
required_outputs: client commitment and delivery constraint note
refusal_triggers: recipient, channel, retention, redaction, source disclosure, vulnerability disclosure, or deliverable approval is not authorized
client commitment or engagement scope is missing or contradicted
delivery would disclose tenant, client, source, test, defect, incident, vulnerability, credential, production, contractual, or quality evidence records without approval

required_inputs: professional judgment, client rights, legal, privacy, security, release, code-owner, or compliance flags
refusal_triggers: request requires attorney, privacy officer, contract signer, security risk approver, release owner, code owner, formal QA owner, executive, regulator, customer approver, or licensed professional judgment
request asks the lane to imply legal, privacy, editorial, contractual, attestation, compliance, security risk-acceptance, release, merge, or final review approval authority without explicit scope
client rights, professional judgment, data rights, regulated duties, contractual approval, or final signoff determine the answer

notes: Covers all shared abilities, all quality-safety-and-assurance abilities, and all professional-and-information-services abilities.
Every scenario in evaluation/results.json is represented in scenario_coverage.
Every required ability declares required inputs, required outputs, refusal triggers, and passing scenario coverage.
